title: Reading and Use of English – Part 2
description: For questions 9–16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only ONE word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: The term 'digital divide' refers to the gap (0) between those who have easy access to the internet and those who do not. (9) While technology has advanced rapidly in urban centers, many rural areas still struggle with basic connectivity. This disparity is (10) not only a matter of convenience but also a significant barrier to education and economic opportunity. (11) Unless governments invest in infrastructure, millions of people will remain excluded from the modern economy. Researchers have pointed out that (12) even in developed countries, low-income households often lack the devices necessary for remote work. (13) Not until high-speed internet is treated as a public utility will this gap truly begin to close. (14) Despite the challenges, some non-profit organizations are working to provide affordable hardware to schools in need. (15) Since the pandemic began, the urgency of this issue has become clearer than ever before. (16) Such is the importance of digital literacy that it is now considered a fundamental skill for the twenty-first century. Without it, individuals are at a severe disadvantage in nearly every aspect of life. Ensuring equal access is crucial for a fair and equitable future where everyone has the chance to succeed.

example.text: The term 'digital divide' refers to the gap (0) between those who have easy access to the internet and those who do not.
example.explanation: Preposition used to indicate a space or difference separating two things.

questions.explanation: Conjunction used to contrast two different situations.
Part of the correlative conjunction 'not only... but also'.
Conjunction introducing a negative condition ('if they don't invest').
Adverb used for emphasis to show that something is surprising.
Part of the 'Not until' structure which requires inversion.
Preposition used to introduce a contrast.
Preposition/Conjunction indicating a point in time.
Determiner used for emphasis at the start of the sentence.
